items.title: Update model comparisons and hero text in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the misleading 'cheaper than direct' claim from the hero subtitle across all locales and introduces a feature flag for the 'vs official' price comparisons. When the flag is off, only AceData's prices will be displayed, ensuring compliance with Stripe's guidelines. The changes enhance clarity and maintain a clean user experience while allowing internal testing of the comparison feature.

items.title: Added third-party trademark notice in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a third-party trademark notice in the site footer, ensuring compliance with Stripe regulations. The notice is now available in all 12 locales, clearly stating that third-party product names and logos are used for identification purposes only. This change enhances transparency and aligns with industry standards.

items.title: Reorder Models and Update Services Label in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The top navigation has been updated to place **Models** as the first item, followed by **Services**. Additionally, the zh-CN label for Services has been simplified from 「所有服务」 to 「服务」, while the English label remains unchanged. These changes enhance the visibility of the Models section on the developer portal.

items.title: Removed Dataset Type Tab from Services Directory in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update eliminates the Dataset service-type tab from the public services directory, ensuring compliance with Stripe regulations. The change also removes 'dataset' from the SEO keywords, effectively closing the last public access point to Dataset services. All Dataset services remain private at the database level, enhancing intellectual property protection.

items.title: Enhance WeChat 4.0 Group Management with Invite/Remove Features in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability to invite and remove group members in WeChat 4.0, along with a new rename functionality. The changes include UIA-driven interactions for member management and improved reliability in UI handling. Extensive testing has been conducted to ensure the robustness of these new features.

items.title: Exciting Update: Added nano-banana-2-lite Model in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `nano-banana-2-lite` model to both the nanobanana and OpenAI CLIs, enhancing the model offerings. Key changes include updates to the model lists in the respective CLI output files and adjustments to the test cases to reflect the new model count. This addition aims to improve user experience by providing more options for image generation.

items.title: Introducing nano-banana-2-lite option in Nexior 🍌
items.summary: This update adds the `nano-banana-2-lite` model option to the Nano Banana model selector, enhancing user choice. Resolution controls are now limited to `nano-banana-2` and `nano-banana-pro`, while stale cached resolutions for 1K-only model requests have been removed. Additionally, Beachball change files have been updated to reflect these changes.

items.title: Expose nano-banana-2-lite for Enhanced Image Generation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `nano-banana-2-lite` model to the Nano Banana and OpenAI Images OpenAPI enums, allowing for cost-effective image generation at the 1K-only tier. Documentation has been updated to reflect these changes, including pricing rules and service introductions. Validation tests confirm that the new model integrates smoothly with existing functionalities.

items.title: Enhance BananaRouter with Nano-Banana-2-Lite Routing in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces public aliases for `nano-banana-2-lite` in the BananaRouter, allowing for improved routing capabilities. The validator now enforces a 1K-only behavior and routes lite traffic exclusively to designated channels in the failover-router configurations. Key tests have been updated to validate the new routing functionality.

items.title: Update x402 Tutorials to Use New Endpoint in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the x402 payment-flow tutorials to point to the new dedicated endpoint `x402.acedata.cloud`, replacing the previous `api.acedata.cloud` for tokenless requests. Key documentation files have been updated to reflect this change, ensuring users have accurate information for utilizing the x402 payment flow. The general API documentation remains unchanged, preserving the integrity of token-authenticated references.

items.title: Update SDK Documentation for New Endpoint in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update points the X402Client SDK documentation, examples, and test scripts to the new dedicated endpoint `x402.acedata.cloud`, replacing the previous `api.acedata.cloud`. Key changes include modifications to READMEs, demo documentation, and various test scripts to ensure compatibility with the new endpoint while keeping the SDK core unchanged. This transition enhances the security and clarity of x402 payment requests.

items.title: Stripe Payments Temporarily Disabled in Frontend Web 🚫
items.summary: The Stripe payment option has been visually disabled and is no longer selectable due to temporary issues with our Stripe account. A warning alert has been added to inform users to use alternative payment methods. This update includes changes to multiple language files to ensure the message is communicated effectively across different languages.

items.title: Enhance Group Chat Creation for WeChat 4.0 in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes group chat creation issues in WeChat 4.0 by implementing a deep-walk method to access the UI tree, allowing for proper interaction with the new Quick Actions menu. Key changes include the ability to locate and select contacts using CheckBox states and a more flexible window-relative layout. The implementation has been validated with live tests and comprehensive mock tests, ensuring robust functionality.

items.title: Enhance Docker Push Reliability in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a retry mechanism for the Docker image push process in the deploy workflow, addressing timeout issues that caused previous deployment failures. By setting the `COMPOSE_HTTP_TIMEOUT` and `DOCKER_CLIENT_TIMEOUT` to 600 seconds and implementing a three-attempt retry loop, we aim to ensure smoother deployments and prevent outages related to Docker image pushes. This change mirrors a successful strategy already in use in the PlatformBackend workflow.

items.title: Enhance x402 Payment Challenge Handling in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces host-gating for x402 payment challenges, ensuring that only allowlisted hosts receive a 402 response for tokenless requests. Other hosts will return a standard 401, preventing exposure of the crypto-payment surface. Key changes include modifications to the Kong custom-auth plugin and the introduction of a new X402_HOSTS setting for better control over payment challenge responses.

items.title: Revamp PayPal Feature Control in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update transitions PayPal from a legacy environment flag to a dynamic FeatureFlag system, enhancing control over its availability. The legacy settings have been removed, and a server-side guard has been implemented to ensure that both the UI and API are synchronized in enabling or disabling PayPal. This change improves security and streamlines feature management.

items.title: Clean Up WeChat4 UI Codebase in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update removes dead code related to the coordinate/OCR send and open driver from the WeChat4 UI module, streamlining the codebase. Key functions and imports that are no longer used have been deleted, and the remaining code is focused solely on the login flow. The changes have been validated with a full mock suite, ensuring no functional impact on existing features.

items.title: Enhance Motion Control with Model Version Selector in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a model version selector for Kling Motion Control, allowing users to choose between Kling 2.6 and Kling 3.0. The selector updates the per-second pricing display based on the selected model, enhancing the user experience and ensuring accurate billing. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for all 18 locales.

items.title: Enable PayPal Payment Option in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the PayPal payment option as a backup for Stripe in the production environment by setting the `FEATURES_ENABLE_PAYPAL` flag to true. This change allows the frontend to display the PayPal button and route payments through the PayBackend service, ensuring continuity during Stripe outages. Careful deployment is required to avoid exposing a non-functional PayPal option to users.

items.title: Enhance Payment Options with PayPal Integration in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update mounts PayPal REST credentials as a backup payment option in both production and test environments. The changes ensure that the PayPal integration can be utilized when Stripe encounters issues, enhancing payment reliability. The deployment includes necessary secret configurations to support this functionality.

items.title: Enhance File Sending via UIA in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update completes the migration of file sending functionality to UI Automation (UIA) for WeChat 4.0. Key changes include the introduction of a new method for sending files, which integrates seamlessly with the existing chat input field. The update also ensures robust validation and error handling, enhancing the overall reliability of file transfers.

items.title: Enhance Pricing Model for Kling Motion Control in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a model version dimension to Kling Motion Control billing, aligning it with official retail pricing and ensuring a ~15% margin on the largest top-up package. Key changes include updated billing rates for different model versions, allowing for more accurate pricing based on the model used. The default model now provides lower costs for callers, while explicit requests for newer models reflect their true pricing.

items.title: Revamping Nexior's Cost Estimation Flow in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a two-step process for cost estimation in Nexior, enhancing user interaction. Users will now receive a confirmation prompt before generating tasks, which will only run after a separate 'start' action. Additionally, outdated static cost displays have been removed to streamline the experience.

items.title: Enhance Maestro API with Estimation and Cleanup in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `maestroOperator.estimate()` and `start()` APIs, allowing for a two-phase estimation process for tasks. Additionally, the old static cost display in the ConfigPanel has been removed, streamlining the user experience by relying on the new estimation workflow. These changes set the stage for future interactive features.

items.title: Implement Two-Phase Estimate Workflow in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a two-phase task workflow for estimates in the Platform Service, allowing users to transition from estimate to park before generating. Key changes include the addition of a new endpoint for creating startable tasks and improvements to task handling to prevent premature billing. The implementation also addresses critical bugs and ensures that parked tasks are not reclaimed or billed incorrectly.

items.title: Enhance WeChat 4.0 Conversation Handling in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update migrates the conversation opening and sending functionality in WeChat 4.0 to a more reliable UI Automation (UIA) method, eliminating the previous coordinate and OCR dependencies. Key changes include the introduction of deterministic methods for opening conversations and sending messages, along with improved error handling for flaky UI reads. The enhancements ensure that messages are sent only when the correct chat is open, significantly improving user experience.

items.title: Enhance Motion Command with New API Features in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update synchronizes the CLI's motion command with the latest OpenAPI specifications, introducing new options for model selection and watermark configuration. Key changes include the addition of `--model-name` and `--watermark-info` options, enhancing user flexibility and functionality. Comprehensive tests have been added to ensure the reliability of these new features.

items.title: Update Motion Control Pricing to Per-Second Rates in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update modifies the pricing display for the Motion Control feature in Nexior to reflect a per-second billing model instead of a fixed estimate. The changes include updates to the `MotionPanel.vue` and `Consumption.vue` components, ensuring users see accurate pricing based on video duration. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for all 18 locales to communicate this new pricing structure effectively.

items.title: Enhance OpenAPI Spec with Model and Watermark Info in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds two new parameters, `model_name` and `watermark_info`, to the `/kling/motion` OpenAPI specification, ensuring documentation parity with the upstream Kling API. Additionally, matching zh-CN translation keys have been introduced. These changes enhance the clarity and usability of the API for developers.

items.title: Enhance Kling Motion Control with New Params in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `model_name` and `watermark_info` parameters to the Kling Motion Control API, ensuring it aligns with the upstream API. The changes allow users to specify the model version and request watermarked results, addressing gaps identified in customer reports. The implementation is safe, with careful exclusion of sensitive parameters to prevent cross-tenant data leaks.

items.title: Unblock Android Production Promotion & iOS Review Submission in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves critical issues preventing the automatic release of Android and iOS applications. For Android, the dispatch process has been modified to promote builds from beta to production, ensuring new releases are successfully deployed. On the iOS side, the submission process has been enhanced to handle existing editable versions, allowing for smoother app review submissions.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI Checker Recovery Mechanism in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new recovery mechanism for the OpenAI checker, allowing it to recover from a demoted state after a specified number of consecutive successful checks, regardless of the current failure score. This change addresses a critical issue where a healthy upstream account was incorrectly marked as invalid due to prolonged outages, ensuring more reliable account validation. The implementation includes additional tests to verify the new functionality and prevent future regressions.

items.title: Enable Free Cost Estimates in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: The /maestro/estimates API has been added to the SKIP_AUTH_API_IDS, allowing users to access cost estimates without balance checks or billing while retaining user identity for rate limiting. This change ensures that the endpoint is fully operational and verified, returning a successful response with an estimate ID. The integration with Kong routing is also live, enhancing the overall functionality.

items.title: Enhance PR Guidelines for Follow-Up Commits in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces critical guidelines in the AGENTS.md document regarding follow-up commits on merged or closed PR branches. It emphasizes the importance of checking the PR status before pushing changes to avoid potential issues with the main branch's history. These changes aim to streamline the workflow and prevent errors during the review process.

items.title: Documentation Sync for Reseller-Wholesale Changes in Index 📚
items.summary: This update synchronizes documentation for the reseller-wholesale implementation, correcting previous misalignments. Key changes include renaming 'authorized_application' to 'sponsor_application' and updating design documents to reflect new binding rules for superusers. The modifications ensure clarity and consistency across the documentation without altering any underlying code logic.

items.title: Enhance Reseller Pool Functionality in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant enhancements to the reseller pool functionality, including the atomic double-ledger mechanism to ensure consistent transactions. Key changes include the addition of new utility functions for settlement modes and improved transaction handling in the application update process. Comprehensive tests have been added to validate these changes, ensuring robust performance and reliability.

items.title: Enhance Reseller Authorization Logic in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new reseller authorization pointer within the application model, allowing for a flexible association between user applications and reseller pools. Key changes include the addition of a nullable foreign key for authorized applications, a new settlement mode utility, and enhanced security measures for the authorization endpoint. The migration and serializer updates ensure that normal user behavior remains unchanged while enabling reseller functionalities.

items.title: Enhance WeChat 4.0 Search with DB Support in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new DB-backed search functionality for WeChat 4.0, allowing for efficient and deterministic contact and group searches. The implementation includes a case-insensitive search across various contact fields and maintains the existing 3.x search behavior. Comprehensive tests have been added to ensure reliability and performance.

items.title: Enhance WeChat 4.0 Sending with UIA Driver in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a robust UIA send driver for WeChat 4.0, replacing fragile coordinate clicks with reliable input controls. Key features include a fail-closed title gate that ensures messages are only sent to the correct chat, enhancing accuracy and safety. Extensive testing confirms functionality and reliability, paving the way for future enhancements.

items.title: Enhance Narration Experience with Voice Selector in Nexior 🎤
items.summary: This update introduces a narration voice selector to the Maestro config panel, featuring a dropdown of nine curated voices along with an audio preview option. Key changes include updates to the Maestro constants, models, and the ConfigPanel component, ensuring a seamless user experience. The addition of internationalization support enhances accessibility across 17 locales.

items.title: Enhance Maestro OpenAPI with Voice Enum in Backend 🎤
items.summary: This update introduces an optional **voice** parameter to the Maestro Videos OpenAPI, featuring 9 curated preset keys for narration timbre. It also supports a raw 32-hex Fish reference_id. This change aligns with the recent updates in PlatformService and the Nexior UI, ensuring a more versatile user experience.

items.title: Enhance voice timbre with language-agnostic presets in Platform Service 🎤
items.summary: This update decouples voice timbre from language, allowing the same Fish reference_id to produce audio in multiple languages. New language-agnostic presets have been introduced, including various male and female voice options. The registry has been updated to remove per-voice language specifications, and tests have been adjusted accordingly, ensuring robust functionality across different languages.

items.title: Introducing Bottom-Up Cost-Estimate API in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a new API endpoint, `POST /maestro/estimates`, which provides users with a detailed cost estimate before placing an order. The implementation includes a planner agent that predicts the cost based on actual API usage and a bottom-up pricing mechanism that retrieves real-time service costs from AceData Cloud. This feature enhances user experience by allowing for informed decision-making without incurring charges for estimates.

items.title: Enhance WeChat 4.0 Messaging with Sender Info in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces message direction and sender identity for WeChat 4.0, enhancing user experience by resolving sender names from the decrypted WCDB. Key changes include the addition of `real_sender_id`, `sender_username`, and `sender_name` in message handling, allowing for clearer differentiation between self and friend messages. Comprehensive tests confirm the functionality across various scenarios, ensuring robust performance.

items.title: Enhance WeChat 4.0 with Real Names and Contacts in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant enhancements to WeChat 4.0 by integrating real conversation names and contact information from the decrypted WCDB. The `list_conversations()` and `list_contacts()` functions now return accurate names and types, improving user experience. Additionally, the changes ensure robust validation through extensive testing, confirming the functionality of the new features.

items.title: Pin WeChat Version to Prevent Auto-Updates in Wisdom 🚫
items.summary: This update modifies the startup script to block WeChat's auto-update CDN, ensuring that the installed version 4.0.6.33 remains intact. The change is idempotent and self-healing, allowing existing installations to revert to the pinned version on the next boot. Importantly, messaging functionality remains unaffected as only the update hosts are null-routed.

items.title: Enhance Blank Render Detection in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a new threshold for detecting near-black renders in the maestro delivery gate. By adding a mean brightness check, videos that are predominantly black will now be rejected during preflight, preventing them from being delivered as successful outputs. The changes include refactoring existing checks into a unit-testable helper function and implementing regression tests to ensure reliability.

items.title: Enhance Narration Options with User-Selectable Voices in Platform Service 🎤
items.summary: This update introduces a new optional `voice` parameter in Maestro, allowing users to select from a curated catalog of professional voices for narration. The implementation includes validation and normalization functions to ensure safe input, while maintaining backward compatibility for existing clients. Comprehensive tests have been added to ensure functionality and stability.
